commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The component lacks any interactive states (hover effects on the card or forecast items) which would enhance the user experience.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover transition to the forecast items or the card wrapper to indicate interactivity.

issues.description: The layout of the metrics is clean, but the forecast strip could benefit from a slightly more refined grid or spacing to ensure uniform alignment regardless of text length.
issues.fix: Ensure the forecast strip columns have a defined width or flex-basis to prevent shifting if text varies.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and correctly maps all props with sensible defaults. It adheres strictly to the design system primitives, though adding minor interactive flair and ensuring consistent alignment in the forecast strip would elevate it from good to production-ready.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid structure and good use of design tokens, but lacks visual polish (no hover/focus states, transitions, or loading states) and the forecast strip could have responsive issues on smaller screens due to `justify="between"` with potentially many items. The layout is generally clean but spacing consistency could be improved with semantic token application.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The legend is incomplete compared to the mapping in getCategoryColor, missing lanthanides and actinides.
issues.fix: Include all categories from the getCategoryColor map in the Legend render loop.

issues.description: The periodic table grid lacks a focus or hover state on the individual cells, making it feel less interactive.
issues.fix: Add a CSS hover effect on the element card wrapper (e.g., scale slightly or darken the background) to indicate it is clickable.

issues.description: Non-matched elements are still interactive via the button inside the dimmed box.
issues.fix: Disable the button element using the disabled attribute when 'isMatch' is false, or set pointer-events: none on the wrapper.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and follows the layout requirements accurately. It uses design tokens effectively and maintains clean component logic. Adding a few refinements to the legend coverage and interactive visual feedback would elevate it to a production-ready state.

evaluation.critique: The component correctly implements a CSS Grid periodic table with proper element positioning and responsive spacing using design tokens. However, there are fallback hex values mixed with design tokens in getCategoryColor(), and the minHeight hardcoded pixel value (64px) should use spacing tokens instead. Polish is adequate but could benefit from focus states on the element buttons and visual feedback on selection.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The component lacks a transition or animation effect when switching between steps.
issues.fix: Wrap the step content in a CSS transition group or use a simple opacity fade animation.

issues.description: The 'Next' button validation logic is only implemented for Step 1, leaving Step 2 and 3 without explicit guardrails if needed.
issues.fix: Add a validation check for Step 2 (e.g., ensure role is selected) to mirror the pattern used in Step 1.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and follows design system principles effectively. It handles state management cleanly and provides a clear user flow, though adding subtle transition animations would significantly improve the polished feel of the wizard.

evaluation.critique: The component has a well-structured layout with proper spacing and responsive design. While it uses design tokens consistently throughout, there are a few hardcoded fallback values (e.g., '24px', '#52525b') that could be problematic if tokens aren't available. Polish is good with validation and disabled states, though loading/transition states could be enhanced.

Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The WeatherDetails component hardcodes 'km/h' in the JSX, ignoring the unit prop and the formatted value provided by the useComponent hook.
issues.fix: Use the formatted wind string from the hook props instead of hardcoding 'km/h' in WeatherDetails.tsx.

issues.description: Weather emojis/icons lack ARIA labels or roles, making them inaccessible to screen readers.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a span with role='img' and aria-label matching the condition text.

issues.description: Lack of hover states or transitions on the card or forecast items.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover background color or scale transformation to ForecastItem and the main Card.

issues.description: The useComponent hook defines a local 'icons' map instead of using the WEATHER_ICONS object exported in constants.ts.
issues.fix: Import and use WEATHER_ICONS from constants.ts inside the useComponent hook.

evaluations.critique: The component has a very strong visual hierarchy and excellent use of layout primitives. The separation of logic into a custom hook is professional, though the implementation falls short by hardcoding units in the sub-components and missing accessibility attributes for the weather icons.

issues.description: The WeatherDetails component hardcodes 'km/h' in the JSX, ignoring the unit prop and the formatted value provided by the useComponent hook.
issues.fix: Use the formatted wind string from the hook props instead of hardcoding 'km/h' in WeatherDetails.tsx.

issues.description: Weather emojis/icons lack ARIA labels or roles, making them inaccessible to screen readers.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a span with role='img' and aria-label matching the condition text.

issues.description: Lack of hover states or transitions on the card or forecast items.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover background color or scale transformation to ForecastItem and the main Card.

issues.description: The useComponent hook defines a local 'icons' map instead of using the WEATHER_ICONS object exported in constants.ts.
issues.fix: Import and use WEATHER_ICONS from constants.ts inside the useComponent hook.

evaluation.critique: The component has a very strong visual hierarchy and excellent use of layout primitives. The separation of logic into a custom hook is professional, though the implementation falls short by hardcoding units in the sub-components and missing accessibility attributes for the weather icons.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The implementation is truncated and incomplete. The main component (PeriodicTable), Search input, and Legend implementation are missing.
issues.fix: Provide the main entry point component that assembles the elements, search bar, and legend.

issues.description: Category key mismatch: 'constants.ts' uses spaces (e.g., 'alkali metal') while 'ElementCell.tsx' and 'DetailsPanel.tsx' expect hyphenated keys (e.g., 'alkali-metal'). This will break color-coding.
issues.fix: Standardize category naming or normalize strings (e.g., .replace(/ /g, '-')) before lookups.

issues.description: Element cells are non-semantic (Box) and lack keyboard interactivity.
issues.fix: Use a button element or add tabIndex={0} and an onKeyDown handler to the Box to allow keyboard selection.

issues.description: The 'isDimmed' logic for search filtering is implemented in types/props but the actual filtering logic in 'useComponent' returns a filtered array instead of dimming non-matches.
issues.fix: Decide between removing non-matches or dimming them; if dimming, the filter logic should return the full list with a 'match' flag.

evaluations.critique: The visual design of the individual element cells is high-quality, with good use of transitions, scaling, and design tokens. However, the component is significantly incomplete (missing the main layout and search UI) and suffers from logic inconsistencies regarding category string formatting.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid design token usage and reasonable hierarchy, but has incomplete implementation (cut-off FormField, missing step components 2 & 3), lacks interactive polish (no transition states on toggles/selects, minimal focus indicators), and has some hardcoded pixel values (32px circles) that should use tokens. Data presentation relies on passed props appropriately but the component is not fully functional.

violations: Hardcoded string "🧑‍💻" found — should be a prop default
Hardcoded string "Developer" found — should be a prop default
Hardcoded string "Designer" found — should be a prop default
Hardcoded string "Manager" found — should be a prop default
Hardcoded string "Other" found — should be a prop default
